question.text: What is the penalty range for insider trading under Section 15G of the SEBI Act?

answer.text: Minimum ten lakh rupees, maximum twenty-five crore rupees or three times the profits made, whichever is higher
Minimum one lakh rupees, maximum one crore rupees
Minimum five lakh rupees, maximum ten crore rupees
Minimum twenty-five lakh rupees, maximum fifty crore rupees

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15G prescribes a penalty not less than ten lakh rupees, extendable to twenty-five crore rupees or three times the amount of profits made out of insider trading, whichever is higher. **The Trap:** The one lakh minimum applies to less severe violations like information filing failures (15A). Insider trading attracts significantly higher penalties reflecting its severity. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The "three times profits" provision ensures that insider trading can never be profitable even after paying the penalty — the penalty exceeds any possible gain.
